skill: One-variable data: Distributions and measures of center and spread
stem: The scores for class X are: 3, 4, 4, 5, 5, 6, 7, 7, 9. The scores for class Y are: 5, 6, 6, 7, 7, 7, 8, 9, 9, 10, 10. The median of the scores for class Y is how much greater than the median of the scores for class X?
rationale: Class X has 9 scores; the median is the 5th value when sorted, which is 5. Class Y has 11 scores; the median is the 6th value, which is 7. The median for class Y is 7 − 5 = 2 greater than the median for class X.
